text:
Once upon a time in the sleepy town of Evergreen, there lived an odd ball named Mildred. Mildred didn’t do things the ordinary way. She had a collection of antique oddities; a talking duckfeathered pen, a talking parrot with a crooked beak, and a collection of feathers she claimed were dinosaurs. She said they could talk sometimes. Her bedroom walls were decorated with vibrant psychedelic posters.

One chilly morning, Mildred stood before her full-length mirror admiring herself. She had a patchwork outfit on – mismatched socks, something akin to a pirate's vest, and what she described as a vestige of vestrumbed owl feathers.

"I haven’t sung in ages," she pondered aloud. She grabbed her feathered feather duster and hollered.

There wasn’t a bird within miles; just odd noises, like someone munching on sunflower seeds.

Mildred marched downstairs, singing loudly.

In her short time, she hadn’t
